page: Amazon Web Services m7g.8xlarge is a general-purpose cloud server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V1 CPU model running at 2.6 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography enabled. The server is provisioned with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong memory subsystem capabilities. The server achieves a top-tier rating in the PassMark memory score and strong results in large-block read and write memory bandwidth tests. Multi-core performance is also strong, as demonstrated by Geekbench multi-core and PassMark CPU physics benchmarks. Database operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the strong category. However, single-core CPU performance, software compilation, and cryptography tests fall within the average range.

Tradeoffs are evident in specialized workloads. While the server handles medium-sized LLM inference at average speeds, text generation using smaller models is poor, and prompt processing for large 70B models is weak. The lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage. Given these characteristics, the m7g.8xlarge is highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, database operations, and static web serving, offering a cost-efficient profile for resource-dense, memory-intensive tasks that do not require local disk storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m7g.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core processing capabilities, particularly in database operations, static web serving, and physics simulations. However, single-core performance and specific LLM text generation tasks are average to poor. The instance offers a balanced resource profile, making it a cost-efficient choice for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and database workloads that leverage ARM-based architecture without requiring local SSD storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.8xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong memory and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.8xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network speed.
tagline: A dedicated arm64 processor instance delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and balanced multi-core performance for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 arm64 cores
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM and 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core benchmark results
No local storage or GPU acceleration included
Best suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded applications
